items.situation: two friends figuring out a bus or train schedule for a day trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Are we still taking the 9:15 train to the coast tomorrow, or should we grab the earlier one?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The 9:15 works for me, but I checked the schedule and it only runs on weekdays. Tomorrow's Saturday.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, right. I completely forgot about that.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The weekend option leaves at 10:40 instead, and it's a bus, not a train.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's fine, as long as we still get there before noon. Does it stop anywhere on the way?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just once, at the harbor stop, for about five minutes. We'll still make it by eleven-thirty.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Great, let's meet at the station at ten then.

questions.stem: What does the man tell the woman about the 9:15 train?

options.C: It stops at the harbor for five minutes
options.D: It was cancelled due to construction

questions.explanation: 男生说9:15的火车“只在工作日运行，明天是周六”，所以这趟车周末不开。B对话中没有提到预留座位；C“在码头停靠五分钟”指的是周末的巴士，不是9:15的火车，属于张冠李戴；D从未提到施工取消。

questions.stem: What will the woman and man most likely do?

options.A: Take the 9:15 train as originally planned
options.B: Postpone the trip until next weekend
options.C: Meet at the station at ten to catch the weekend bus
options.D: Drive to the coast instead of taking public transit

questions.explanation: 女生最后说“那我们十点在车站见”，呼应男生提到的10:40周末巴士，所以他们会去赶那趟巴士。A是他们已经排除的方案，因为9:15周末不开；B对话中完全没提到推迟行程；D两人从未讨论开车。

items.situation: two friends debating whether a new cafe is worth the price

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did you end up trying Bloom Café near the library?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, yesterday. The latte was great, but six dollars for a small cup felt steep.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Six dollars? Wow, that's more than the place downtown.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I know. The pastries are cheaper though, only two dollars each, and they're actually pretty filling.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, maybe I'll just get a pastry and skip the coffee.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's probably the smart move. I'll stick to downtown for my coffee from now on.

questions.stem: How much does a pastry cost at Bloom Café?

options.A: Six dollars, the same as the latte
options.D: Two dollars more than downtown's price

questions.explanation: 男生说“糕点便宜多了，只要两块钱一个”，所以答案是B。A把拿铁的价格错误地安到了糕点上；C对话中从未提到免费赠送；D是编造的对比说法，对话没有说贵两块钱。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do at Bloom Café?

options.A: Order both a latte and a pastry
options.B: Continue buying her coffee downtown instead
options.D: Order a pastry without buying coffee

questions.explanation: 女生说“也许我就买个糕点，不买咖啡了”，所以她最可能只点糕点。A与她“跳过咖啡”的决定相反，是她已经放弃的选项；B是男生自己打算做的事，被错误地安到了女生身上；C对话没有说她要彻底不去这家店。

items.situation: two students talking about a pet or houseplant one is caring for

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How's the basil plant you're watching for your neighbor doing? You mentioned it wasn't looking great last week.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, it got worse. The leaves started turning yellow, so I moved it away from the window.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yellow leaves usually mean overwatering, not too much sun. Did you check the soil before watering?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not really, I just watered it every morning like she told me to.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's probably the problem. Let it dry out a few days, then only water when the soil's dry.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Okay, I'll hold off and check the soil first. Thanks, I don't want to kill her plant before she's back.

questions.stem: What does the woman think caused the plant's yellow leaves?

options.B: A cold draft near the window
options.D: The neighbor giving him incorrect plant food

questions.explanation: 女生说“叶子发黄通常是浇水太多，而不是晒太阳太多”，所以她认为原因是浇水过勤。A正好说反了，她明确排除了这个原因；B对话中从未提到窗边有冷风；D从未提到施肥的问题。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.A: Wait a few days before watering the plant again
options.B: Ask the neighbor to take the plant back early
options.C: Repot the basil plant in fresh soil
options.D: Continue watering it every morning as before

questions.explanation: 男生最后说“好，我先不浇水，先检查土壤”，采纳了女生的建议，所以他会先等几天再浇水。B对话中完全没有提到提前归还植物；C没有讨论换盆；D是他之前的做法，正是被建议改掉的，属于已被否定的选项。

items.situation: two friends deciding whether to buy concert tickets now or wait

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The Nova Sound concert tickets go on sale tonight at eight. Should we buy them right when they drop?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'd rather wait. Prices sometimes drop closer to the date if it doesn't sell out.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: True, but this venue is small, only four hundred seats. It could sell out in an hour.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Four hundred? Okay, that changes things. What time does the box office open online?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Eight sharp, and mobile-only orders start ten minutes early through the app.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let's just download the app now and be ready right at eight.

questions.stem: How many seats does the venue have?

options.B: An unlimited number of standing-room spots
options.C: Four hundred seats, but only for members

questions.explanation: 女生明确说“这个场地很小，只有四百个座位”，所以答案是D。A把数字改成了两百，是错误细节；B对话没有提到站票区；C编造了“仅限会员”这一限制，对话中并未出现。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do tonight?

options.A: Wait until closer to the concert date to buy tickets
options.B: Download the ticket app before eight o'clock
options.C: Call the box office to reserve seats by phone
options.D: Convince the woman to skip the concert

questions.explanation: 男生最后说“我们现在就下载app，八点准时准备好”，所以他会在八点前下载app。A是他一开始的想法，后来因为座位少而放弃了；C对话没有提到打电话预订；D没有任何迹象表明他想放弃看演唱会，属于过度推断。

items.situation: two students swapping music recommendations because one is bored of the same pop singers

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I've been stuck on the same five pop artists for months. I need something different.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Try jazz. My sister got me into it, and it's nothing like the radio stuff.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Jazz always seemed intimidating, like I wouldn't know where to start.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Start upbeat, not ballads. A trio called Blue Hour plays free shows at the coffeehouse Fridays.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Free? Low-risk then, if I hate it I haven't lost any money.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. If you like it, I'll send a playlist with their studio tracks.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Deal, I'll meet you there Friday.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: Trying a new music genre to escape a listening rut
options.B: Finding a free concert to attend this week
options.C: Convincing the man to stop listening to jazz
options.D: Learning to play a musical instrument together

questions.explanation: 对话核心是女生厌倦了同样的流行歌手，男生建议她尝试爵士乐这个新曲风，因此主旨是“尝试新的音乐类型摆脱单调”。B只是次要细节（免费演出），不是主要话题；C与实际情况相反，是男生在向女生推荐爵士乐，而不是女生劝男生放弃；D对话中完全没有提到学乐器。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do on Friday?

options.A: Purchase a jazz album instead of going to the show
options.B: Ask her sister to introduce her to jazz
options.C: Attend Blue Hour's free performance at the coffeehouse
options.D: Quit listening to pop music entirely

questions.explanation: 女生最后说“说定了，我周五在那儿见你”，指的就是去咖啡馆看Blue Hour的免费演出，所以答案是C。A与她的计划相反，她选择的正是免费演出而不是花钱买专辑，这是被排除的选项；B把男生的姐姐错误地安在了女生身上；D对话没有说她要彻底放弃流行音乐，属于过度推断。
